name: trulens-dashboard

metadata.name: trulens-dashboard
metadata.summary: Library to systematically track and evaluate LLM based applications.

documentation:
# trulens-dashboard

## Install Node.js

See [the Node.js website](https://nodejs.org/en/download/package-manager) for details

## Building

Build with `python -m build`

functions.name: trulens.dashboard.streamlit.init_from_args
documentation:
Parse command line arguments and initialize Tru with them.

As Tru is a singleton, further TruSession() uses will get the same configuration.

functions.name: trulens.dashboard.streamlit.trulens_leaderboard
documentation:
Render the leaderboard page.

Args:

    app_ids List[str]: A list of application IDs (default is None)

Example:
    ```python
    from trulens.core import streamlit as trulens_st

    trulens_st.trulens_leaderboard()
    ```

functions.name: trulens.dashboard.streamlit.trulens_feedback
documentation:
Render clickable feedback pills for a given record.

Args:

    record: A trulens record.

Example:
    ```python
    from trulens.core import streamlit as trulens_st

    with tru_llm as recording:
        response = llm.invoke(input_text)

    record, response = recording.get()

    trulens_st.trulens_feedback(record=record)
    ```

functions.name: trulens.dashboard.streamlit.trulens_trace
documentation:
Display the trace view for a record.

Args:

    record: A trulens record.

Example:
    ```python
    from trulens.core import streamlit as trulens_st

    with tru_llm as recording:
        response = llm.invoke(input_text)

    record, response = recording.get()

    trulens_st.trulens_trace(record=record)
    ```

functions.name: trulens.dashboard.run.find_unused_port
documentation: Find an unused port.

functions.name: trulens.dashboard.run.run_dashboard
documentation:
Run a streamlit dashboard to view logged results and apps.

Args:
    port (Optional[int]): Port number to pass to streamlit through `server.port`.

    address (Optional[str]): Address to pass to streamlit through `server.address`. `address` cannot be set if running from a colab notebook.

    force (bool): Stop existing dashboard(s) first. Defaults to `False`.

    sis_compatibility_mode (bool): Flag to enable compatibility with Streamlit in Snowflake (SiS). SiS runs on Python 3.8, Streamlit 1.35.0, and does not support bidirectional custom components. As a result, enabling this flag will replace custom components in the dashboard with native Streamlit components. Defaults to `False`.

    _dev (Path): If given, runs the dashboard with the given `PYTHONPATH`. This can be used to run the dashboard from outside of its pip package installation folder. Defaults to `None`.

    _watch_changes (bool): If `True`, the dashboard will watch for changes in the code and update the dashboard accordingly. Defaults to `False`.

Returns:
    The [Process][multiprocessing.Process] executing the streamlit dashboard.

Raises:
    RuntimeError: Dashboard is already running. Can be avoided if `force` is set.

functions.name: trulens.dashboard.run.stop_dashboard
documentation:
Stop existing dashboard(s) if running.

Args:
    force: Also try to find any other dashboard processes not
        started in this notebook and shut them down too.

        **This option is not supported under windows.**

Raises:
        RuntimeError: Dashboard is not running in the current process. Can be avoided with `force`.

params.name: Doc("Seconds to wait for dashboard to start")

variables.name: trulens.dashboard.constants.CACHE_TTL
documentation: 15 minutes

functions.name: trulens.dashboard.Leaderboard.render_leaderboard
documentation:
Renders the Leaderboard page.

Args:
    app_name (str): The app name to render the leaderboard for.

functions.name: trulens.dashboard.components.record_viewer.record_viewer
documentation:
Create a new instance of "record_viewer", which produces a timeline

Args:
    record_json: JSON of the record serialized by `json.loads`.

    app_json: JSON of the app serialized by `json.loads`.

Returns:
    Start time of the selected component in the application. If the whole app is selected,
Create a wrapper function for the component. This is an optional
best practice - we could simply expose the component function returned by
`declare_component` and call it done. The wrapper allows us to customize
our component's API: we can pre-process its input args, post-process its
output value, and add a docstring for users.

modules.name: trulens.dashboard
documentation: WARNING: This file does not follow the no-init aliases import standard.

functions.name: trulens.dashboard.ux.components.write_or_json
documentation:
Dispatch either st.json or st.write depending on content of `obj`. If it is
a string that can parses into strictly json (dict), use st.json, otherwise
use st.write.

functions.name: trulens.dashboard.ux.components.draw_calls
documentation: Draw the calls recorded in a `record`.

methods.name: trulens.dashboard.appui.AppUI.__init__
documentation: very prototype

functions.name: trulens.dashboard.pages.Records._render_record_metrics
documentation:
Render record level metrics (e.g. total tokens, cost, latency) compared
to the average when appropriate.

functions.name: trulens.dashboard.pages.Records.render_records
documentation:
Renders the records page.

Args:
    app_name (str): The name of the app to render records for.

functions.name: trulens.dashboard.display.get_icon
documentation:
Get the icon for a given feedback definition and result.

Args:
    fdefThe feedback definition.

    result: The result of the feedback.

Returns:
    str: The icon for the feedback

functions.name: trulens.dashboard.display.get_feedback_result
documentation:
Retrieve the feedback results including metadata (such as reasons) for a given feedback name from a TruLens record.

Args:
    tru_record: The record containing feedback and future results.
    feedback_name: The name of the feedback to retrieve results for.

Returns:
    pd.DataFrame: A DataFrame containing the feedback results. If no feedback
        results are found, an empty DataFrame is returned.

functions.name: trulens.dashboard.display.highlight
documentation:
Apply background color to the rows of a DataFrame based on the selected feedback.

Args:
    row (pandas.Series): A row of the DataFrame to be highlighted.
    selected_feedback (str): The selected feedback to determine the background color.
    feedback_directions (dict): A dictionary mapping feedback names to their directions.
    default_direction (str): The default direction for feedback.

Returns:
    list: A list of CSS styles representing the background color for each cell in the row.

functions.name: trulens.dashboard.display.expand_groundedness_df
documentation:
Expand the groundedness DataFrame by splitting the reasons column into separate rows and columns.

Args:
    df (pd.DataFrame): The groundedness DataFrame.

Returns:
    pd.DataFrame: The expanded DataFrame.

classes.name: trulens.dashboard.ux.styles.CATEGORY

class_variables.name: trulens.dashboard.ux.styles.CATEGORY.ALL
documentation: not including UNKNOWN intentionally

Feedback result categories for displaying purposes: pass, warning, fail, or
unknown.

functions.name: trulens.dashboard.pages.Compare.render_app_comparison
documentation:
Render the Compare page.

Args:
    app_name (str): The name of the app to display app versions for comparison.

functions.name: trulens.dashboard.utils.records_utils.df_cell_highlight
documentation:
Returns the background color for a cell in a DataFrame based on the score and feedback name.

Args:
    score (float): The score value to determine the background color.
    feedback_name (str): The feedback name to determine the background color.
    feedback_directions (dict): A dictionary mapping feedback names to their directions. True if higher is better, False otherwise.
    n_cells (int, optional): The number of cells to apply the background color. Defaults to 1.

Returns:
    A list of CSS styles representing the background color.

functions.name: trulens.dashboard.utils.records_utils.display_feedback_call
documentation:
Display the feedback call details in a DataFrame.

Args:
    record_id (str): The record ID.
    call (List[Dict[str, Any]]): The feedback call details, including call metadata.
    feedback_name (str): The feedback name.
    feedback_directions (Dict[str, bool]): A dictionary mapping feedback names to their directions. True if higher is better, False otherwise.

functions.name: trulens.dashboard.utils.records_utils._render_feedback_pills
documentation:
Render each feedback as pills.

Args:
    feedback_col_names (Sequence[str]): The name of the feedback function columns.
    feedback_directions (Dict[str, bool]): A dictionary mapping feedback names to their directions. True if higher is better, False otherwise.
    selected_row (Optional[pd.Series], optional): The selected row (if any). If provided, renders the feedback values. Defaults to None.

Returns:
    Any: The feedback pills streamlit component.

modules.name: trulens.dashboard.utils.sis_utils
documentation: This module contains utility functions for rendering the dashboard on Streamlit in Snowflake.

functions.name: trulens.dashboard.utils.dashboard_utils.read_query_params_into_session_state
documentation:
This method loads query params into the session state. This function should only be called only once when the page is first initialized.

Args:
    page_name (str): Name of the page being initialized. Used to prefix page-specific session keys.
    transforms (Optional[dict[str, Callable]], optional): An optional dictionary mapping query param names to a function that deserializes the respective query arg value. Defaults to None.

functions.name: trulens.dashboard.utils.dashboard_utils.is_sis_compatibility_enabled
documentation:
This method returns whether the SIS compatibility feature is enabled.
The SiS compatibility feature adapts dashboard components to support Streamlit in Snowflake (SiS).
As of 11/13/2024, SiS runs on Python 3.8, Streamlit 1.35.0, and does not support bidirectional custom components.

In the TruLens dashboard, this flag will replace or disable certain custom components (like Aggrid and the trace viewer).

Returns:
    bool: True if the SIS compatibility feature is enabled, False otherwise.

functions.name: trulens.dashboard.utils.dashboard_utils.get_session
documentation:
Parse command line arguments and initialize TruSession with them.

As TruSession is a singleton, further TruSession() uses will get the same configuration.
